signals.headline: Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at +2
signals.raw:
Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at +2.7% | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Germany's inflation rate rose to +2.7% in March 2026 from +1.9% in February, the highest since January 2024 (+2.9%), driven by sharp increases in energy prices like motor fuel and heating oil amid the Iran war.[1][4] This official release from Destatis on April 10 exceeds prior expectations of +2.7% and could shift predict

signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at +2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at +2.2% | YES | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% in prior months, below market forecasts of 2.4%, with Statistics Korea data released around April 22.[2][4] This beat on downside amid Iran conflict energy volatility may influence Bank of Korea rate bets, as it aligns near the 2% target.[2]

signals.headline: Market impact**: Confirmation of the ceasefire and imminent talks would shift prediction markets toward de-escalation be
signals.raw: Market impact**: Confirmation of the ceasefire and imminent talks would shift prediction markets toward de-escalation bets on Iran-US peace, Hormuz reopening (easing ~20% global oil transit), and Middle East stability, outweighing April 12 reports of faltering Islamabad talks[7][1][3][5]. US has not fully detailed terms beyond the ceasefire; White House response pending[1].
